q: Azure Container Apps is built on which open-source technologies (abstracted from the user)?
e: ACA runs on AKS infrastructure with KEDA scaling, Dapr building blocks, and Envoy ingress — without exposing the K8s API.

q: A team wants microservices with autoscaling and service discovery but has no Kubernetes expertise and doesn't need the K8s API. Recommend:
e: ACA gives K8s-grade capabilities as a serverless platform; AKS is for teams needing full API control.

q: When must you choose AKS over Container Apps?
o: You need custom CRDs/operators, node-level control, or a specific service mesh
e: ACA hides the K8s API — custom operators, DaemonSets, node customization require AKS.

q: What is a Container Apps environment?
o: An isolation boundary sharing VNet and Log Analytics for a set of apps
e: Apps in one environment share networking (optionally your VNet) and logging; internal environments keep ingress private.

q: How do you run a gradual rollout sending 10% of traffic to a new app version in ACA?
o: Deploy a second app and DNS round-robin
Multiple revision mode with traffic splitting 90/10 across revisions
e: Revisions are immutable versions; traffic weights across revisions enable canary/blue-green.

q: Which technology powers ACA scale rules, including scaling on Service Bus queue length?
e: KEDA scalers drive event-driven scaling (Service Bus, Event Hubs, Kafka, cron, HTTP concurrency, CPU/memory).

q: What is the cost benefit of setting minReplicas to 0?
o: Scale to zero — no compute cost when idle (with cold-start tradeoff)
e: Consumption workloads can scale to zero; latency-sensitive apps often keep min ≥ 1 to avoid cold starts.

q: A nightly batch container must run to completion on a schedule inside the same environment as your apps. Use:
e: ACA Jobs support manual, scheduled (cron), and event-driven run-to-completion workloads.

q: Which Dapr building block would ACA microservices use for pub/sub over Service Bus without SDK-level coupling?
o: Dapr pub/sub component backed by Service Bus
e: Dapr pub/sub abstracts the broker; the component config points at Service Bus, keeping app code portable.

q: Best-practice authentication for ACA pulling images from Azure Container Registry:
e: Managed identity + AcrPull avoids registry credentials entirely.

q: How should an internal-only microservices platform on ACA be exposed to other private workloads?
o: Internal environment (VNet) with internal ingress and private DNS
e: Internal environments place ingress on a private IP in your VNet; consumers resolve via private DNS.

q: Which ACA feature lets one environment host both serverless apps and dedicated-compute (or GPU) apps?
e: Workload profiles combine Consumption and Dedicated (D/E-series, GPU) profiles in a single environment.

q: To control ACA egress traffic through Azure Firewall you should…
o: Deploy a workload profiles environment in your VNet and apply UDRs to the firewall
e: Workload-profile VNet environments support UDRs for firewall-controlled egress.

q: A single short-lived container needs to run for an isolated build task, no orchestration. Cheapest, simplest service:
e: ACI runs single containers on demand per-second billing — no orchestration overhead.

q: Where do ACA apps get secrets at runtime following best practice?
o: Key Vault references resolved via managed identity (or app secrets)
Plain env vars in source control
e: Key Vault-backed secret references with managed identity keep secrets centralized and rotated.

q: What does ACA session affinity do?
o: Routes a client's requests to the same replica (sticky sessions)
e: Sticky sessions help stateful web workloads; stateless design remains preferable.

q: An HTTP API on ACA should scale based on concurrent requests per replica. Which scale rule?
e: The HTTP scaler targets concurrent requests per replica — the natural rule for synchronous APIs.

q: Which choice is best for a team needing web app hosting with deployment slots, easy certs/domains, and no containers required?
e: App Service is the PaaS web host with slots and integrated domain/cert management.

q: Which flow should a single-page application use to sign in users?
e: Auth code + PKCE replaced implicit flow for SPAs — tokens via secure code exchange.

q: A background daemon with no user context needs to call an API. Which flow?
e: Client credentials issues app-only tokens; prefer certificates, managed identity, or federation over secrets.

q: API A receives a user's token and must call downstream API B as that user. Which flow?
e: OBO exchanges the incoming user assertion for a token to the downstream API preserving user identity.

q: Which token should a client present to a protected API?
e: Access tokens are for APIs; ID tokens are for the client's own authentication of the user.

q: An API validating a JWT must check which claims at minimum?
o: Signature, issuer (iss), audience (aud), expiry (exp), then scopes/roles
e: Full validation: signature against JWKS, iss, aud, exp/nbf, then authorize via scp/roles claims.

q: In Entra tokens, what indicates delegated permissions vs application permissions?
e: scp carries delegated scopes with user context; roles carries app roles for app-only tokens (or user role assignments).

q: Application (app-only) permissions on Microsoft Graph require what before use?
e: Application permissions always need admin consent; delegated ones may too depending on scope.

q: A smart-TV app needs user sign-in but has no browser/keyboard. Which flow?
e: Device code shows a code the user enters on another device to complete sign-in.

q: Why is ROPC (resource owner password credentials) discouraged?
o: App handles raw passwords, breaks MFA/Conditional Access, no federation
e: ROPC is legacy-only: incompatible with modern security controls and passwordless.

q: What is the difference between an app registration and an enterprise application (service principal)?
o: Registration is the global app definition; the service principal is its instance/identity in a tenant
e: One registration (in the home tenant) can have SPs in many tenants (multi-tenant apps).

q: Your customer-facing product needs social logins, custom-branded sign-up, and consumer identity at scale. Use:
o: Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM, successor to B2C)
e: External ID is Microsoft's CIAM offering for consumer sign-up/sign-in and social identities.

q: Partner-company employees need access to your internal app using their own credentials. Use:
e: B2B guests federate partners into your tenant, governed by access reviews and entitlement management.

q: Which Entra feature enforces 'require MFA when sign-in risk is high, block legacy auth'?
e: Conditional Access evaluates signals and applies controls; Identity Protection supplies risk scores.

q: Best way for APIM to enforce that only tokens containing the Orders.Read scope reach the orders API:
e: Gateway-level claim checks reject unauthorized calls early; backend still re-validates (defense in depth).

q: Should the backend API skip token validation because APIM already validated the JWT?
o: No — zero trust: every hop validates; network position is not identity
e: Assume breach: backends must independently validate tokens even behind a gateway.

q: Which mechanism gives an AKS pod or external workload an Entra identity without storing any secret?
o: Client secret in a K8s secret
e: Federation trusts the platform's OIDC token and exchanges it for Entra tokens — no stored credentials.

q: For time-boxed, anonymous-ish download access to a single blob for an external user, which is appropriate?
e: SAS remains right for constrained delegation; user-delegation SAS is signed with Entra credentials — safer than account keys.

q: High-assurance service-to-service calls between APIM and a payments backend should add which transport control?
o: Mutual TLS (client certificates) plus network isolation
e: mTLS authenticates both ends at transport layer, layered with token auth and private networking.

q: What are the three guiding principles of Zero Trust?
o: Verify explicitly; use least-privilege access; assume breach
e: These principles drive per-request verification, JIT/JEA access, and segmentation/telemetry.

q: A multi-tenant SaaS API must accept tokens from many customer tenants. What must its token validation handle?
o: Issuer validation per allowed tenant (tid) with common metadata endpoint
e: Multi-tenant apps validate the issuer pattern/tenant allowlist since iss varies per tenant.

q: Where should refresh tokens for a confidential web app be kept?
o: Server-side, protected (e.g., encrypted session/store) — never exposed to JS
e: Confidential clients keep tokens server-side; SPAs rely on MSAL cache with short-lived tokens instead.

q: Which Key Vault capability supports automatic rotation stories and eliminates app restarts for secret updates?
o: Versioned secrets with rotation policies and apps referencing latest via MI
Export to env file at build
e: Rotation policies plus managed-identity retrieval of current versions enables zero-touch rotation.
